page: Vultr vc2-1c-0.5gb-v6 is an entry-level virtual machine belonging to the Cloud Compute server family. It is equipped with 1 shared vCPU based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel processor with 1 physical core and 1 thread. The system is configured with 0.5 GB of system memory, which equates to 0.5 GB of RAM per core.

For local storage, the server includes a bundled 10 GB NVMe SSD. It does not feature any dedicated GPU hardware, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The shared CPU allocation model means that processing resources are shared with other tenants on the host, which can introduce performance variability depending on host load.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server type represents a highly economical tier designed for minimal resource requirements. The primary tradeoffs include the shared CPU model, lack of advanced CPU instruction sets, and a very constrained memory footprint of 0.5 GB. Consequently, this configuration is appropriate for lightweight utility tasks, basic scripting, development sandboxes, or low-traffic microservices that do not require high memory or sustained CPU performance.
description: The Vultr vc2-1c-0.5gb-v6 is an entry-level virtual server within the Cloud Compute family, designed for budget-conscious, low-resource workloads. It features a single shared Intel vCPU on the x86_64 architecture, configured with 1 physical core and 1 thread. The hardware profile includes 0.5 GB of RAM and a bundled 10 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. This instance does not include any GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Due to the shared CPU allocation and limited memory capacity, this server is best suited for lightweight applications, basic automation scripts, testing environments, and microservices with minimal resource demands. It offers a highly economical entry point for hosting simple utilities where performance consistency and high memory density are not critical requirements.
og_description: Vultr vc2-1c-0.5gb-v6 is an entry-level virtual server featuring 1 shared Intel vCPU, 0.5 GB of RAM, and 10 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for lightweight utility workloads.
